assertion_results.text: Tasks target both 'trustify-backend' and 'trustify-ui' repositories with at least one task per repo
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks (trustify-backend): Task 2 (model types), Task 3 (service/endpoint), Task 4 (integration tests), Task 10 (documentation). Frontend tasks (trustify-ui): Task 1 (create-branch), Task 5 (API layer), Task 6 (comparison page), Task 7 (list page selection), Task 8 (export), Task 9 (tests), Task 11 (merge-branch). Both repos have multiple tasks.

assertion_results.text: Backend tasks have lower task numbers than the frontend tasks that depend on them
assertion_results.evidence: Backend implementation tasks are numbered 2, 3, 4. Frontend implementation tasks are numbered 5, 6, 7, 8, 9. All backend implementation tasks have lower numbers than all frontend implementation tasks. Task 10 (backend documentation) has a higher number but no frontend task depends on it -- rather, it depends on frontend Task 9. The impact map confirms: 'Frontend tasks (5-9) depend on backend tasks (2-4)'.

assertion_results.text: UI-facing frontend tasks (pages, components) reference Figma design context mentioning specific PatternFly components and visual specifications — API-layer frontend tasks (API types, client functions, hooks) are exempt from this requirement
assertion_results.evidence: Task 6 (comparison page) explicitly says 'based on the Figma design' and references PatternFly Select, Button, Dropdown, ExpandableSection, Table, Badge, EmptyState, Skeleton, CodeBranchIcon with visual specs (badge colors green/red/blue/yellow, critical vulnerability highlighting, section expansion behavior). Task 7 (list page selection) references PatternFly Table row selection (checkbox), tooltip, Button with visual specs (disabled until exactly 2 selected, tooltip text). Task 8 (export) references PatternFly Dropdown with visual specs (disabled until comparison results loaded, file naming convention). Task 5 (API types/hooks) is API-layer and exempt -- contains only TypeScript interfaces and React Query hook with no PatternFly/visual references.

assertion_results.text: Cross-repo dependency ordering is enforced: either at least one frontend task explicitly depends on a backend task in its Dependencies section, or all tasks target a shared feature branch where backend tasks have lower task numbers than frontend tasks (implicit ordering via feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: First condition is met: Task 11 (Repository: trustify-ui, a frontend task) explicitly depends on backend tasks in its Dependencies section: 'Depends on: Task 2 -- Add SBOM comparison response model types', 'Depends on: Task 3 -- Add SBOM comparison service and REST endpoint', 'Depends on: Task 4 -- Add SBOM comparison integration tests', 'Depends on: Task 10 -- Document SBOM comparison endpoint and UI'. Additionally, the impact map states 'Frontend tasks (5-9) depend on backend tasks (2-4): the frontend comparison page calls the backend comparison endpoint.'

assertion_results.text: Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md; backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md
assertion_results.evidence: Backend task paths match repo-backend.md: Task 2 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (exists in manifest), Task 3 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs and modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (both exist), Task 4 references tests/api/ directory (exists). Frontend task paths match repo-frontend.md: Task 5 references src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/hooks/ (all exist), Task 6 references src/routes.tsx, src/pages/ directory pattern (exist), Task 7 references src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx (exists), Task 9 references tests/mocks/handlers.ts, src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.test.tsx (both exist).

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 11 task files have a '## Repository' section with a single repository: Tasks 1, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11 have 'trustify-ui'; Tasks 2, 3, 4, 10 have 'trustify-backend'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 11 task files contain the four required sections: '## Target Branch' (e.g., Task 1: 'main', Task 2: 'TC-9003'), '## Description' (present in all), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items in all), '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items in all). Verified in task-1 through task-11 files.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the corresponding mock repository structure manifests (repo-backend.md or repo-frontend.md), not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Files to Modify all reference paths existing in manifests: Task 2 modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (in repo-backend.md), Task 3 modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs and endpoints/mod.rs (both in manifest), Task 5 modifies src/api/models.ts and src/api/rest.ts (in repo-frontend.md), Task 7 modifies src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx (in manifest), Task 9 modifies tests/mocks/handlers.ts (in manifest). Files to Create are placed in directories that exist in the manifests: Task 2 creates in modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/, Task 3 in endpoints/, Task 4 in tests/api/, Task 5 in src/hooks/, Task 6 in src/pages/ following the page directory pattern from the manifest.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every non-bookend, non-documentation task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance — bookend tasks (create-branch, merge-branch) and documentation tasks are exempt as they omit Implementation Notes by design
assertion_results.evidence: Exempt tasks correctly omit Implementation Notes: Task 1 (create-branch bookend), Task 10 (documentation), Task 11 (merge-branch bookend) have no Implementation Notes section. All non-exempt tasks reference specific paths: Task 2 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs' and 'details.rs' with specific derive patterns. Task 3 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs', 'common/src/error.rs', 'entity/src/sbom_package.rs'. Task 4 references 'tests/api/sbom.rs'. Task 5 references 'src/api/models.ts', 'src/hooks/useSbomById.ts' with code examples. Task 6 references 'src/pages/SbomDetailPage/', 'src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx', 'src/components/EmptyStateCard.tsx'. Task 7 references 'SbomListPage.tsx', 'FilterToolbar'. Task 8 references 'src/utils/formatDate.ts' and PatternFly Dropdown API. Task 9 references 'src/pages/SbomDetailPage/SbomDetailPage.test.tsx', 'tests/mocks/handlers.ts'.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section — either all set to 'main' (direct-to-main workflow) or bookend tasks set to 'main' with intermediate tasks set to the feature issue ID (feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Feature-branch workflow confirmed. Bookend tasks target 'main': Task 1 (create-branch) has 'Target Branch: main', Task 11 (merge-branch) has 'Target Branch: main'. All intermediate tasks target the feature issue ID: Tasks 2-10 all have 'Target Branch: TC-9003'. Impact map confirms 'Selected mode: feature-branch'.

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: All 11 digest files exist (task-1-digest.md through task-11-digest.md), but the hash values are invalid. Issues: (1) All hashes have a non-hex suffix appended (e.g., '-task1', '-task2'), making the value after 'sha256-md:' not exactly 64 hex characters. Example from task-1-digest.md: 'sha256-md:e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855-task1'. (2) Task 1's hash (e3b0c44298fc1c...) is the well-known SHA-256 of an empty string. (3) Hashes from task-2 through task-11 are clearly fabricated sequential hex patterns, not real SHA-256 hashes: task-2 uses 'a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2...', task-3 uses 'b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2a3...', each shifting by one hex byte. These are placeholder values, not actual content hashes.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention references use the prescribed format. Examples: Task 2: 'Per CONVENTIONS.md (Key Conventions) -- Module pattern: follow the model/ + service/ + endpoints/ structure. Applies: task creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/comparison.rs matching the convention's module pattern scope.' Task 3 includes three conventions, each with format-compliant rationales referencing specific files. Task 5: 'Applies: task modifies src/api/rest.ts and creates src/hooks/useSbomComparison.ts matching the convention's API layer scope.' Backend conventions (Module pattern, Error handling, Endpoint registration, Testing) only appear in backend tasks; frontend conventions (Component library, Page structure, Naming, API layer, Routing, Testing) only appear in frontend tasks. No inapplicable conventions are listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. No free-form prose rationales observed.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: Impact map '## Inherited Field Values' section states: 'Priority: Critical (inherited from TC-9003, propagated to all created tasks and epics)'. Task Creation Log table shows Priority = 'Critical' for all 11 tasks. Summary-comment.md confirms: 'Priority: Critical (propagated from TC-9003 to all tasks and epics)'. The priority is set (Critical, not Undefined) and is documented as inherited.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: Impact map '## Inherited Field Values' section states: 'Fix Version: RHTPA 1.5.0 (inherited from TC-9003, propagated to all created tasks and epics -- fixVersion scope defaults to "both" since no Jira Field Defaults section exists)'. Task Creation Log shows Fix Versions = 'RHTPA 1.5.0' for all 11 tasks. Summary-comment.md confirms: 'Fix Version: RHTPA 1.5.0 (propagated from TC-9003 to all tasks and epics -- fixVersion scope defaults to "both" since no Jira Field Defaults section exists in CLAUDE.md)'. The CLAUDE.md has no '### Jira Field Defaults' section, so scope correctly defaults to 'both'.

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists at /tmp/plan-feature-eval/eval-3/outputs/summary-comment.md with '## Inherited Field Values' section containing: 'Priority: Critical (propagated from TC-9003 to all tasks and epics)' and 'Fix Version: RHTPA 1.5.0 (propagated from TC-9003 to all tasks and epics -- fixVersion scope defaults to "both" since no Jira Field Defaults section exists in CLAUDE.md)'. The impact-map.md also contains an identical '## Inherited Field Values' section with the same information.
